content.problemStatement:
You start off with initially array A=[0]A = [0]A=[0].

You need to handle MMM operations of 222 types:


Ti=1T_i = 1Ti​=1 : Let X=MEX⁡(A)X = \operatorname{MEX}(A)X=MEX(A) †^{\dagger}†, replace AAA with [X]+A[X] + A[X]+A.
Ti=2T_i = 2Ti​=2 : Replace AAA with A+AA + AA+A.
In both the above cases, +++ denotes array concatenation.

After each operation, find the number of inversions‡^{\ddagger}‡ in the array AAA. It is guaranteed that the length of the array AAA never exceeds 10910^9109.

†^{\dagger}† The MEX⁡\operatorname{MEX}MEX of an array is the smallest non-negative integer not present in the array. For example, MEX⁡([0,0,1,3])=2\operatorname{MEX}([0, 0, 1, 3]) = 2MEX([0,0,1,3])=2 and MEX⁡([1,2])=0\operatorname{MEX}([1, 2]) = 0MEX([1,2])=0.

‡^{\ddagger}‡ The number of inversions in the array AAA is the number of pairs (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) such that 1≤i<j≤N1 \le i < j \le N1≤i<j≤N and Ai>AjA_i > A_jAi​>Aj​.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line contains MMM - the number of operations.
The second line contains MMM integers - T1,T2,…,TMT_1, T_2, \ldots, T_MT1​,T2​,…,TM​.




Output Format
For each test case, print MMM integers, the number of inversions after each operation.
constraints: The length of the array does not exceed 10910^9109.
The sum of MMM over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : Here is how the array changes:

After 1st1^{st}1st operation, A=[1,0]A = [1, 0]A=[1,0]. There is 111 inversion.
After 2nd2^{nd}2nd operation, A=[1,0,1,0]A = [1, 0, 1, 0]A=[1,0,1,0]. There are 333 inversions.
After 3rd3^{rd}3rd operation, A=[2,1,0,1,0]A = [2, 1, 0, 1, 0]A=[2,1,0,1,0]. There are 777 inversions.
After 4th4^{th}4th operation, A=[2,1,0,1,0,2,1,0,1,0]A = [2, 1, 0, 1, 0, 2, 1, 0, 1, 0]A=[2,1,0,1,0,2,1,0,1,0]. There are 222222 inversions.
